description: Canonical registry of MCP servers used across the FlexNetOS meta workspace.

servers.summary: Multi-repo operations for the meta workspace: project listing, cross-repo git, build/test orchestration, code search, dependency/impact analysis, and snapshots.
Rust-native agent-to-agent session mesh. Lets coding-agent sessions message each other and pushes live nudges into a peer's tmux/zellij pane.
Infinite Context Memory — persistent cross-session memory for agents: recall/store/update memories, topic hygiene, memoirs, feedback, and transcripts.
Text-to-speech for spoken agent feedback: speak summaries, listen (vox_hear), manage voices/packs/clones, and configure backend.

Build and manage n8n workflows from an agent. 7 core node-documentation/validation tools work with no credentials; 13 management tools (create/update/run workflows, executions, credentials) require an n8n API URL + key.
servers.notes: Fork of czlonkowski/n8n-mcp (TypeScript, MIT). Runs via `npx n8n-mcp` or the ghcr.io/czlonkowski/n8n-mcp Docker image. Not a meta workspace member.

servers.summary: Up-to-date, version-specific library/framework documentation and code examples on demand (resolve-library-id + query-docs). Avoids stale training-data answers for SDKs, CLIs, and APIs.
servers.notes: Third-party (Upstash, MIT). Launched with `bunx` because the meta-managed JS runtime is Bun and the bundled `npx` is non-functional. Get a key at context7.com; pass it via the `${CONTEXT7_API_KEY}` env-ref (do not commit the literal key).

servers.summary: Browser automation against the user's own running Chrome/Edge via the 'Playwright MCP Bridge' extension (--extension mode): navigate, click, type, snapshot, read console/network, and drive real authenticated tabs — no separate headless browser.
servers.notes: Third-party (Microsoft, Apache-2.0). Requires the 'Playwright MCP Bridge' Chrome/Edge extension installed; copy its per-profile token into `${PLAYWRIGHT_MCP_EXTENSION_TOKEN}` (env-ref, never commit the literal) to skip the per-connection approval dialog. Launched with `bunx` because the bundled `npx` is non-functional in the meta env.

servers.summary: Cloudflare developer platform operations (Workers, KV, R2, etc.) via the claude.ai OAuth connector.
Read designs from Figma into code and write designs into Figma (Code Connect, screenshots, diagrams, design systems).
Gmail threads, drafts, and labels via the claude.ai OAuth connector.
Google Calendar events, availability, and scheduling via the claude.ai OAuth connector.
Google Drive file search, read, and management via the claude.ai OAuth connector.
Hugging Face Hub: models, datasets, papers, spaces, and docs via the claude.ai OAuth connector.
Microsoft 365: Outlook mail/calendar, Teams chat, and SharePoint search via the claude.ai OAuth connector.
Notion pages, databases, comments, and search via the claude.ai OAuth connector.
GitHub repository, issue, PR, and Actions operations from an agent — the official GitHub MCP server (Go), run locally via Docker.
servers.notes: Official server (github/github-mcp-server, Go). Canonical run is the Docker image; the .github_org project pins it by digest. Alternatives: the legacy npm `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github` via `bunx`, or the remote OAuth endpoint `https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/`. PAT via the `${GITHUB_TOKEN}` env-ref — never commit the literal token.

servers.summary: Web search and page-content retrieval via Exa's hosted MCP endpoint (nothing runs locally). Core search works unauthenticated; an Exa API key unlocks higher limits and more tools.
servers.notes: Hosted HTTP endpoint at mcp.exa.ai — no local install. For authenticated use, append the key as a query param (`https://mcp.exa.ai/mcp?exaApiKey=…`) via an env-ref; never commit the literal key. Get a key at exa.ai.

servers.summary: Persistent knowledge-graph memory (entities, relations, observations) the agent can read and write across a session.
servers.notes: Official reference server (modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/memory, MIT). Launched with `bunx` (npx is non-functional in the meta env). envctl instead wires a meta-native bash wrapper (`envctl-mcp-memory-server`); the `bunx` form here is the portable canonical. Set `MEMORY_FILE_PATH` to relocate the store.

servers.summary: A single tool for structured, step-by-step reflective reasoning — break a hard problem into revisable thought steps.
servers.notes: Official reference server (modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/sequentialthinking, MIT). Launched with `bunx` (npx is non-functional in the meta env).

servers.summary: n8n's OWN built-in MCP server (Settings → MCP), exposing your workflows as tools over authenticated HTTP. Complements the external `n8n-mcp` node-docs tool.
servers.notes: Distinct from `n8n-mcp` (the external node-documentation/validation tool). Requires a running n8n with MCP access enabled. Authenticated with a Bearer token of audience `mcp-server-api` (NOT a public-api key) carried in the snippet's `Authorization` header via `${N8N_MCP_SERVER_TOKEN}` — keep it in a gitignored settings.local.json, never commit it.

servers.summary: Cognitum cloud control plane (api.cognitum.one) over SSE — 7 cloud tools (catalog, orders, witness chain, etc.). Read access is open; write operations need an API key.
servers.notes: FlexNetOS-related cloud service. Write ops require `${COGNITUM_API_KEY}` (env-ref, never commit). Pairs with `cognitum-seed` (the local appliance); shares its doc/snippet.

servers.summary: Local Cognitum Seed appliance (114 on-device tools) over SSE. Requires a paired bearer token; falls back to the USB-gadget endpoint http://169.254.42.1/mcp (link-local, physical-cable trust).
servers.notes: FlexNetOS Seed USB / secret-manager factor — activates via link-local IPv4 (169.254.42.1:8443) + mDNS, not DHCP. Token via `${COGNITUM_SEED_TOKEN}` (env-ref). Shares its doc/snippet with `cognitum`.
